steps.title: Fit two points from the line
steps.content: The fastest check is to use two easy points from the displayed line of best fit. The line passes near $(0,3.4)$ and $(10,13.4)$, so put those points into a Desmos table.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: The regression row $y_1\sim m x_1+b$ gives $m=1$ and $b=3.4$. So the line should have positive slope $1$ and positive $y$-intercept $3.4$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Only $y=x+3.4$ has both a positive slope and a positive intercept. Choice B has the wrong intercept sign, and choices C and D have negative slope. Therefore choice A is correct.
